fingerprint: B(r) magnetic field equation is a 1D radial projection of the globe's spherical harmonic IGRF model; 'zero fitted parameters' claim hides three IGRF-derived coefficients; two-pole model remains OPEN-011 (unsolved); monitoring is hardcoded identity check
globe_value: IGRF-13 geomagnetic field: defined as spherical harmonic expansion Σ(n,m) [g_n^m cos(mφ) + h_n^m sin(mφ)] P_n^m(cosθ) (a/r)^(n+1), where Legendre polynomials P_n^m arise from separating Laplace's equation in SPHERICAL coordinates. Dipole field at magnetic north pole ~58,000–60,000 nT. Field falls off as (R/r)³ on a sphere.
dome_value: Model page shows single-pole B(r) = 60,000 × exp(−r/8619) nT. Two-pole form B(r_N, r_S) = A × exp(−r_N/λ₁) + C × exp(−r_S/λ₂) is listed as OPEN-011 (under development, not yet solved as of April 2026). Specific coefficients 62376 and 64852 do NOT appear anywhere in the dome repository — they exist only in our review's analysis. The coupling constant κ = 1.67 nT/µGal is hardcoded in both monitor.py and inject_ai_layer.py.
match_precision: Single-pole amplitude 60,000 nT is a round-number approximation of IGRF polar field strength (~58–60 kT); decay constant 8,619 km is reused from firmament height H(r) without independent derivation
why_it_matters: Five distinct findings from code verification: (1) The B(r) equation's functional form (exponential decay on a disc) has no derivation from flat-disc electrodynamics — Maxwell's equations in a cylindrical cavity produce Bessel-function modes (J_m, Y_m), not exponential decay. The exponential is the radial shadow of a dipole 1/r³ field projected onto an AE disc. (2) The two-pole B(r) model (with fitted A and C coefficients) is STILL AN OPEN PROBLEM (OPEN-011 on the model page) — the dome claims WIN-012 as confirmed while the underlying geomagnetic model is explicitly unsolved. (3) The coupling constant monitoring in monitor.py is a pure identity check: kappa_obs = 10.9 / 6.5 (both hardcoded literals, no live data) compared to KAPPA = 1.67 (locked constant). It will always pass. No INTERMAGNET data, no gravimeter data, no live computation of any kind. (4) The decay constant 8,619 km (LAMBDA_G in monitor.py) is repurposed from H(r) = 8537 × exp(−r/8619), the firmament height function. The dome asserts without proof that the magnetic field shares the same decay length as the dome ceiling height — there is no physical justification for why a magnetic field should decay with the same scale as a structural boundary. (5) No repository script anywhere computes B(r) as a function. The formula exists only as text on the model.html page. The single-pole version uses a round number (60,000 nT) rather than a fitted coefficient, and the two-pole version hasn't been implemented at all.
suggested_text: The dome's magnetic field equation B(r) = 60,000 × exp(−r/8619) is not derived from flat-disc electrodynamics. Maxwell's equations in a cylindrical cavity produce Bessel-function radial modes, not exponential decay — the exponential form is what you get when you project a sphere's 1/r³ dipole field onto an azimuthal equidistant disc. The two-pole model needed to handle Southern Hemisphere field strengths remains OPEN-011, unsolved. The coupling constant κ = 1.67 is a hardcoded literal in monitor.py — the 'observed' value (10.9/6.5) is equally hardcoded, with both numerator and denominator as static constants rather than fetched from any data source. No script in the dome repository computes B(r); the formula exists only as display text on the model page.

monitor.py.line_173: LAMBDA_G = 8619.0 (locked constant, shared with H(r) firmament height)
monitor.py.lines_465_476: Domain 7: kappa_obs = 10.9 / 6.5 (BOTH hardcoded literals, NO live data fetch), err_kappa compared to KAPPA, source: 'BOU 2017 eclipse (WIN-012)'

inject_ai_layer.py.win_entries: WIN-012 listed with status: confirmed, data_source: BOU + Mohe

model.html.single_pole: B(r) = 60,000 × exp(−r/8619) nT (round-number coefficient, not fitted)
model.html.two_pole: B(r_N, r_S) = A × exp(−r_N/λ₁) + C × exp(−r_S/λ₂) listed as OPEN-011 (unsolved)
model.html.note: Model page explicitly states two-pole form is under development; 62376 and 64852 do NOT appear

found_in.test_8537.py: Uses H(r) = 8537 × exp(-r/8619) for map projection, NOT magnetic field
found_in.verify_predictions.py: Uses H(r) = 8537 × exp(-r/8619), no magnetic field formula

code_search.notes: The specific B(r) coefficients 62376 and 64852 cited in our previous fingerprint analysis do NOT exist in the dome's repository — they appear only in our own curmudgeon review (SEC-3.5.8). The dome's model page shows a generic two-pole formula listed as OPEN-011 (unsolved). The only implemented magnetic-related computation is the kappa identity check in monitor.py, which compares two hardcoded values (1.67 vs 10.9/6.5). This means the dome claims WIN-012 as 'confirmed' while the underlying geomagnetic model is simultaneously listed as an open problem.

notes: UPGRADED from 0.85 to 0.95 confidence after code verification. Previous analysis correctly identified the structural argument (exponential vs Bessel, IGRF dependency) but INCORRECTLY cited specific coefficients (62376, 64852) as dome parameters. These coefficients do not exist in the dome repo. The dome's actual single-pole B(r) uses 60,000 nT (a round number), and the two-pole model is explicitly OPEN-011. The monitoring is even weaker than previously described: a pure hardcoded identity with no live data. The B(r) formula exists only as model.html text — no computation anywhere. NOTE-003 item 2 (WIN-012) complete.
